summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 5: re-detect against main (37 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (37 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 5 detection: 10 from clusters = 10 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 5 verify: 7 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 2 app-misuse, 1 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Field-level canEdit()/canSee() authorization is never enforced on the write path (mass-assignment bypass)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In validated() (and/or runCreate/runUpdate), after extracting/validating, strip any key whose Field returns false from canBeEditedBy($request->user(), $record) before passing $data to fill()/save() — iterate $resource->effectiveFields(), and for each field with method canBeEditedBy that denies, unset($data[$field->getName()]); mirror the existing FieldSchemaSerializer logic so write-path auth matches the documented "Enforcement is real" contract.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced live against apps/showcase on main. Registered a Resource on the posts table with (new BooleanField('featured'))->canEdit(fn () => false) plus the required title/slug/author_id fields, authenticated as admin, and POSTed featured=1 to /admin/repro-posts. Result: assertSessionHasNoErrors + 302 redirect, the Post was created, and post->featured persisted as true — the field-write-auth assertion fired ('canEdit(false) field was persisted from request'). Code path confirms it: ResourceController::validated() (lines 377-405) builds rules via extractRules() -> FieldRulesExtractor::extract(Resource::effectiveFields()), which reads only getValidationRules() (FieldRulesExtractor.php:29-45) and never consults canBeEditedBy/canBeSeenBy; it returns $request->validate($rules) verbatim. runCreate/runUpdate (Resource.php:254-289) then do $record->fill($data)->save() with no field-auth pruning. The canBeSeenBy/canBeEditedBy oracles are consumed ONLY by FieldSchemaSerializer (render payload readonly/visibility flags) — cosmetic. Arqel\Auth\Concerns\AuthorizesRequests::authorizeField correctly checks canBeEditedBy for the 'edit' op, but grep shows it is never referenced from packages/core/src or packages/form/src — dead code on the write path. This contradicts the explicit HasAuthorization docblock contract ('Enforcement is real', controller prunes/rejects server-side), so it is a real framework bug, not deferred (FORM-007 is FormRequest generation; this is field-level write auth) and not app-misuse. Severity high rather than blocker: exploitation requires a Resource author to actually declare canEdit/canSee predicates (the feature is opt-in and the showcase baseline ships none), but where used it is a genuine authorization/integrity bypass allowing overwrite of read-only/hidden fields.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/fields/src/Concerns/HasAuthorization.php (lines 11-24) documents the contract explicitly: canSee()/canEdit() predicates are "evaluated by the controller before ... accepting an update payload" and "Enforcement is real". But packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php::validated() (lines 377-405) only runs $request->validate($rules) from extractRules(); rules come from Resource::effectiveFields() (FieldRulesExtractor::extract, packages/form/src/FieldRulesExtractor.php) with NO consultation of canBeEditedBy()/canBeSeenBy(). store() (line 64) and update() (line 99) only call $this->authorize('create'/'update') at the resource level. canBeSeenBy/canBeEditedBy are consumed ONLY by FieldSchemaSerializer for the render payload (readonly/canSee flags) — purely cosmetic. REPRODUCED via live HTTP: a Resource with (new BooleanField('featured'))->canEdit(fn () => false); POST /admin/{slug} with featured='1' passed validation, ran runCreate(), and persisted featured=true. Assertion failed: 'canEdit(false) field was persisted from request — field-level write auth is NOT enforced.' Any user shown a read-only or hidden field can submit its value and overwrite it.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: CollabDocumentController REST endpoints have NO record-level authorization — any authenticated user can read/overwrite any model's collaborative draft (cross-tenant leak)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/realtime/src/Http/Controllers/CollabDocumentController.php:27 (show) and :53 (store); routes registered with only ['web','auth'] in packages/realtime/routes/api.php:23
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In show()/store(), resolve the owning model from $resource+$id (mirror AwarenessChannelAuthorizer::resolveModelClass + ResourceRegistry::findBySlug) and call $this->authorize('view', $record) / authorize('update', $record) before reading/writing the snapshot — matching the Gate::check('view',$record) already done on the WebSocket awareness channel and ResourceController::authorize(); 404 when the model/record can't be resolved instead of allow-all.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced on main. Routes GET|POST /admin/{resource}/{id}/collab/{field} are registered live in apps/showcase (verified via `route:list --path=collab -v`: middleware = web, auth ONLY). CollabDocumentController::show() returns base64 of the full Yjs binary state for any {resource}/{id}/{field}; store() overwrites it with optimistic-concurrency only. grep for Gate|authorize|Policy|can|allows|denies|abort|403|view in the controller returns ZERO matches (exit 1). This bypasses the record-level authorization the framework enforces everywhere else: core ResourceController::authorize() gates show with `view` (line 84) and update with `update` (line 94), and the realtime package's OWN channel side (AwarenessChannelAuthorizer line 55-57, ResourceChannelAuthorizer::authorizeRecord line 94) deliberately calls Gate::check('view',$record) for the same collab feature's WebSocket awareness channel. The REST snapshot path is the persistent store for that same protected content, so the missing check is a wiring/authorization gap, not intended design — the controller test only asserts auth (401 unauth) and never asserts any record-level authz, and passes with user id=7 freely reading/writing posts/42. Cross-tenant leak is also real: YjsDocument has no BelongsToTenant/tenant scoping (grep on model+migration = exit 1) and keys by raw URL slug string (model_type = $resource), so there is no morph-class or tenant isolation. Vulnerable code lives in the arqel-dev/realtime framework package (not app code) => framework-bug. Severity high (not blocker): exploit requires an authenticated session and the feature is a documented scaffold, but it enables authenticated cross-user and cross-tenant read+overwrite of draft content and is route-registered in the shipped showcase. Not a deferred/documented-intended exclusion (export signed-URL exclusion does not apply).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/realtime/src/Http/Controllers/CollabDocumentController.php show()/store() and routes/api.php gate the collab snapshot endpoints (`GET|POST /admin/{resource}/{id}/collab/{field}`) with ONLY `['web','auth']`. grep for Gate|authorize|Policy|can|allows|denies|abort|403 in the controller returns ZERO matches. show() returns base64 of the full Yjs binary state for ANY resource/id/field; store() overwrites it. This directly bypasses the carefully Policy-aware authorizers used by the WebSocket side: channels.php delegates to AwarenessChannelAuthorizer/ResourceChannelAuthorizer which call Gate::check('view',$record). The REST snapshot path has no equivalent view/update check. YjsDocument has no BelongsToTenant/tenant scoping (grep on the model + migration = empty), and queries key by `model_type = $resource` (raw URL slug string), so there is not even morph-class isolation. Result: a logged-in low-privilege user (or a user in tenant B) can fetch and overwrite another user's/tenant's draft content. Contrasts sharply with the deliberately Policy-aware channel auth, making this a clear wiring/authorization gap, not intended design.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: All 5 AI field HTTP controllers resolve the field via Resource::fields() instead of effectiveFields() — form()-declared AI fields return spurious 422
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In all 5 controllers replace `method_exists($instance,'fields') ? $instance->fields() : []` with `method_exists($instance,'effectiveFields') ? $instance->effectiveFields() : (method_exists($instance,'fields') ? $instance->fields() : [])`, and add a form-only AI fixture to the test suite to cover the path.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Concretely reproduced in the arqel-dev/ai package's own test harness. I created a Resource declaring an AiTextField ONLY inside form() (empty flat fields()) — the idiomatic layout-aware placement that core Resource::effectiveFields() (Resource.php:169-182) exists to unify. Repro test 1 confirmed the field is found via effectiveFields() but NOT via fields(); repro test 2 hit the real HTTP route POST /admin/form-only-ai/fields/summary/generate and got HTTP 422 'AiTextField [summary] not found' when a correct lookup yields 200 with the generated text. All 5 controllers share the defect: AiGenerateController:45, AiClassifyController:53, AiExtractController:58, AiTranslateController:59, AiAnalyzeImageController:72 all do `$instance->fields()` and loop by name. This is the SAME bug class already fixed in core/fields for issue #94 (FieldUploadController:137 and FieldSearchController:127 use effectiveFields(); FormOnlyUploadingResource fixture is the #94 regression guard), plus ResourceController:441 (validation) and InertiaDataBuilder:314 (rendering) all read effectiveFields() — the 5 AI controllers are the only consumers still reading fields() directly. The AI fixtures (FakeAiResource etc.) declare AI fields exclusively in fields(), so the test suite never exercises the form() path, leaving the gap uncovered. Form::getFields() flattens layout components recursively, so AI fields nested in Sections are returned by effectiveFields(). This is a framework bug, not app-misuse: form()-only field placement is documented as the single source of truth and is a supported, tested idiom in core/fields/form; an app following it correctly gets spurious 422s on every AI endpoint. Severity high (not blocker): AI features are functional when fields are declared in fields(), and a one-token-per-controller fix resolves it; but the idiomatic placement is silently broken, matching the suspected severity.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: AiGenerateController, AiClassifyController, AiExtractController, AiTranslateController and AiAnalyzeImageController all do `$fields = $instance->fields()` then loop for the AiTextField/AiSelectField/AiExtractField/AiTranslateField/AiImageField by name. But core Resource::effectiveFields() (packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:169-182) is the documented single source of truth: when a Resource declares a form() schema it returns `form()->getFields()`, NOT fields(); the whole point (per project memory: 'form() needn't re-declare fields()') is that fields() may be sparse/empty. Live repro (tinker, anonymous Resource with the AiTextField only inside form()->schema([...]) and fields() returning []): `Field found via fields(): false`, `Field found via effectiveFields(): true`, so the controller's lookup misses and every endpoint returns 422 'AiTextField [summary] not found on resource [...]' for the idiomatic form()-only field placement. The AI package fixtures (e.g. FakeAiResource) only ever declare AI fields in fields(), so the test suite never exercises the form() path — gap is uncovered. Validation, rendering and InertiaDataBuilder all read effectiveFields(); these 5 controllers are the odd ones out.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Locale preference never survives session expiry — SetLocaleMiddleware reads arqel_locale cookie but LocaleController never writes it
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In LocaleController::__invoke, queue the cookie alongside the session write — e.g. return back()->withCookie(cookie()->forever('arqel_locale', $locale)); (or Cookie::queue) so the tier-2 cross-session layer the middleware reads is actually populated.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced at source level and confirmed irrefutable. SetLocaleMiddleware (packages/core/src/Http/Middleware/SetLocaleMiddleware.php) documents a 4-tier resolution order whose tier 2 is `cookie('arqel_locale')` — explicitly labeled "preferência cross-session" in the PHPDoc — and actively reads it at line 55. LocaleController::__invoke (the sole writer endpoint, POST /admin/locale, route arqel.locale.update) only does $request->session()->put('locale', $locale) at line 34 and never queues the arqel_locale cookie. A repo-wide grep for 'arqel_locale' (excluding vendor/node_modules) returns ONLY: the middleware read + PHPDoc, and SetLocaleMiddlewareTest which *manually injects* the cookie into a synthetic Request (Request::create('/admin','GET',[],['arqel_locale'=>'pt_BR'])) to prove the middleware READS it — never a production write. The JS LocaleSwitcher (packages-js/i18n/src/LocaleSwitcher.tsx:51) POSTs to the same endpoint via Inertia router and sets no document.cookie either. Therefore the entire user-facing flow writes only session state. Once the Laravel session expires (default SESSION_LIFETIME, or browser close with ephemeral session cookie), locale resolution skips the empty cookie tier and falls through to Accept-Language / defaultLocale, silently discarding the user's explicit choice. This is a genuine framework bug, not app-misuse: showcase/demo/tenant-demo all just consume the framework controller+middleware and have no hook to write the cookie themselves. It is not a deferred/intended feature — the middleware and its test treat the cookie tier as live behavior; only the writer half was never implemented (classic read-without-write gap). Classified framework-bug, isReal=true. Severity medium: degraded UX after session expiry (locale silently reverts) rather than data loss or security impact, and Accept-Language fallback often masks it during the active working window; the writer fix is a one-liner in LocaleController.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: SetLocaleMiddleware (packages/core/src/Http/Middleware/SetLocaleMiddleware.php:55) documents and reads cookie('arqel_locale') as the tier-2 'preferência cross-session'. But LocaleController::__invoke (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/LocaleController.php:34) only does $request->session()->put('locale', $locale) — it never queues the arqel_locale cookie. grep across packages/, packages-js/, apps/ for 'arqel_locale' (excluding vendor/node_modules) finds only the middleware READ and a test input; nothing ever WRITES it. The JS LocaleSwitcher (packages-js/i18n/src/LocaleSwitcher.tsx:51) POSTs to the same /admin/locale endpoint, so the documented cookie-based persistence tier is unreachable through Arqel's own flow: once the session expires, the user's locale choice is lost despite the middleware advertising cross-session cookie persistence.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: XLSX exporter renders date columns as raw Excel serial numbers (e.g. 45306.4375) instead of dates
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Either format dates to strings like the other two exporters (`$value->format('Y-m-d')` in the `'date'` branch of formatCell, lines 124-126), OR attach an OpenSpout Style with `setFormat('yyyy-mm-dd')`/`'yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss'` to date cells (via spatie/simple-excel addRow style arg) so a date numFmt is emitted in styles.xml.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Concretely reproduced end-to-end. Built a real XLSX with a [type=>'date'] column and DateTimeImmutable('2024-01-15 10:30:00'); unzipped it. sheet1.xml cell D2 = <c r="D2" s="0"><v>45306.4375</v></c> (raw Excel serial), and styles.xml has numFmts count="0" with the only cellXfs entry using numFmtId="0" (General). 45306.4375 decodes exactly to 2024-01-15 10:30:00, but under General format Excel/LibreOffice show the literal number, not a date — directly contradicting the class docblock (lines 16-18: "Excel renders them as dates rather than literal strings"). XlsxExporter::formatCell() (lines 123-126) returns the raw DateTimeInterface and relies on OpenSpout to format; OpenSpout's WorksheetManager writes the serial value but applies a date numFmt only when a Style carries one, and none is attached. CsvExporter:118 and PdfExporter:148 both format dates to 'Y-m-d', making XLSX the inconsistent outlier. The existing test XlsxExporterTest.php:99-116 only round-trips through OpenSpout's reader (which heuristically rebuilds a DateTime from the serial), masking the rendering failure. This is the package's own code (framework-bug), on a real user-facing path (ExportAction::withColumns -> export, with 'date' an explicitly supported/tested type), and is unrelated to deferred EXPORT-007/008 signed-URL work. Severity medium: wrong/ugly display, not data loss/crash/security; the value is recoverable and limited to XLSX date columns.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/export/src/Exporters/XlsxExporter.php::formatCell() returns the raw DateTimeInterface instance for type='date' columns (lines 123-126), relying on spatie/simple-excel + OpenSpout to render it as a native date. I built a real XLSX end-to-end (XlsxExporter::export with a column [type=>'date'] and a DateTimeImmutable('2024-01-15 10:30:00')) and unzipped it: cell D2 is written as <c r=\"D2\" s=\"0\"><v>45306.4375</v></c> — the Excel serial-date number — but styled with s=\"0\" (the default General format). styles.xml confirms numFmts count=\"0\" and the only cellXfs entry has numFmtId=\"0\" (General). I traced OpenSpout: WorksheetManager writes DateTimeCell as <v>DateHelper::toExcel(...)</v> and AbstractStyleManager::applyExtraStylesIfNeeded() ONLY handles wrap-text for newlines — it does NOT auto-apply a date numFmt. So with no Style carrying a date format, Excel/LibreOffice display the literal serial number 45306.4375, NOT a date. This directly contradicts the class docblock ('Excel renders them as dates rather than literal strings', lines 16-18) and is inconsistent with CsvExporter/PdfExporter which format dates to 'Y-m-d'. The existing test XlsxExporterTest.php:99-114 only round-trips through OpenSpout's *reader* (which heuristically reconstructs a DateTime from the serial), masking the real-world rendering failure. Fix: apply an OpenSpout Style with setFormat('yyyy-mm-dd' or 'yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss') to date cells, or format dates to strings like the other two exporters.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: MCP prompts emit non-spec-compliant PromptMessage.content (array instead of single ContentBlock), breaking prompts/get for real MCP clients
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In both prompt generators, change 'content' => [['type'=>'text','text'=>$promptText]] to 'content' => ['type'=>'text','text'=>$promptText] (single ContentBlock object). Update PHPDoc return type on generate() (content array<...> -> array{type,text}) and fix the codifying tests (PromptsRegistrationTest.php:49, ReviewResourcePromptTest.php:37/39, MigrateFilamentResourcePromptTest.php:38/40, McpConversationTest.php:247) to assert ['content']['text'] instead of ['content'][0]['text'] — matching the already-correct McpServerTest.php:247 shape.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed and reproduced on main (files identical to main; no pending fix). The authoritative MCP spec (modelcontextprotocol 2025-11-25, fetched via context7: schema.mdx PromptMessage + server/prompts.mdx prompts/get example) defines PromptMessage.content as a SINGLE ContentBlock object {type,text}, e.g. "content": {"type":"text","text":...}. Both bundled prompt generators instead emit content as an array of blocks: ReviewResourcePrompt.php:103-105 and MigrateFilamentResourcePrompt.php:108-110 ('content' => [[...]]). McpServiceProvider.php:106/115 registers generate(...)['messages'] as the generator, and McpServer::getPrompt() (line 427-432) passes messages straight through, so prompts/get responses for review_resource and migrate_filament_resource ship content as a JSON array — which spec-conforming clients (Claude Desktop) reject/mis-parse. I ran the generator live: emitted content is array_is_list==true with numeric index [0] (non-compliant). The McpServer core itself is NOT the bug: its own test (McpServerTest.php:230/247) registers a generator returning the correct single-object content and asserts ['content']['text'], proving the server is shape-agnostic and supports the spec shape — only the two bundled generators are wrong. tools/call correctly returns content as an array (McpServer.php:340, spec-compliant for CallToolResult). The suite codifies the wrong shape (PromptsRegistrationTest.php:49 and the two Unit prompt tests assert ['content'][0]['text']), so all 27 tests pass locally while the real-client contract is broken. This is a genuine framework bug in shipped feature code (MCP-008), not a deferred/intended behavior. Severity medium: the two prompts are unusable against conforming clients, but it is a bundled-feature shape mismatch on the prompts surface only — no crash, security, or data-loss impact, and tools/resources are unaffected.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Both prompt generators return content wrapped in an extra array: packages/mcp/src/Prompts/ReviewResourcePrompt.php:101-107 ('content' => [['type'=>'text','text'=>...]]) and MigrateFilamentResourcePrompt.php (same pattern, line 108). The MCP spec (verified via context7 against modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol schema + server/prompts docs) defines PromptMessage as { role: Role; content: ContentBlock } where content is a SINGLE object {type,text} — NOT a list. The official prompts/get example shows \"content\": { \"type\": \"text\", \"text\": ... } (object, not array). McpServer::getPrompt() (packages/mcp/src/McpServer.php:415-433) passes the generator's messages straight through, so prompts/get responses for review_resource and migrate_filament_resource ship content as an array of blocks. Claude Desktop / spec-conforming MCP clients will fail to parse or reject these messages. Note: tools/call result content IS correctly an array (McpServer.php:339-343, spec-compliant there) — only the prompts path has the wrong shape. The test suite (McpConversationTest.php:247 asserts messages[0]['content'][0]['text']) codifies the wrong shape so it passes locally while breaking against real clients.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: BroadcastStateTransitionListener passes the Model class (not the Resource class) to ResourceUpdated — workflow broadcasts land on a model-name channel that diverges from the registered Resource slug
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In handle(), resolve the Resource via the registry — `$resourceClass = app(ResourceRegistry::class)->findByModel($record::class) ?? $record::class;` — and pass that as the first arg to ResourceUpdated::dispatch(), so the slug comes from the registered Resource's getSlug() instead of the Model basename fallback.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning:
The mechanism is confirmed by reading the code, not just plausible. (1) BroadcastStateTransitionListener::handle (line 56-60) passes `$record::class` — the Eloquent Model FQCN — as ResourceUpdated's $resourceClass. (2) ResourceUpdated::resolveSlug() (line 84) only calls getSlug() when method_exists($resourceClass,'getSlug'); an Eloquent Model has no getSlug(), so it falls to the basename fallback Str::of(class_basename)->beforeLast('Resource')->snake('-')->plural(). (3) The canonical path BroadcastsResourceUpdates::afterUpdate (line 51-55) instead passes static::class (the Resource subclass), whose getSlug() returns the registered $slug. (4) StateTransitioned carries only the Model (no Resource reference) and the listener has no registry injected, so as written it genuinely cannot derive the correct slug. Therefore, for any Resource whose $slug diverges from class_basename(Model)->kebab->plural, workflow state-transition broadcasts land on a model-named channel while the Resource/subscriber side uses the registered slug — a real channel-name divergence and dropped notifications.

This is classified framework-bug (the defect is entirely inside arqel-dev/realtime's framework code; no app misuse). Severity is LOW, not medium: it does NOT reproduce in the dogfood baseline. All four showcase Resources (Ticket→'tickets', Post→'posts', Author→'authors', Setting→'settings') set $slug to exactly the value the basename fallback derives, so both channels coincide and no message is lost. The claim's 'live repro' relied on $slug='support-tickets' for model Ticket, which exists nowhere in the showcase — it is a hypothetical, not an observed baseline failure. The bug is latent: it manifests only when a consumer picks a custom slug that differs from the model name (a legitimate, supported use of the slug system), so it is a genuine correctness gap worth fixing but not currently breaking the dogfood app.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/realtime/src/Workflow/BroadcastStateTransitionListener.php::handle() does `ResourceUpdated::dispatch($record::class, $record, $event->userId)`. But ResourceUpdated::resolveSlug() expects the FIRST arg to be a Resource subclass and calls `$this->resourceClass::getSlug()`; an Eloquent Model has no getSlug(), so it silently falls through to the basename fallback `Str::of(class_basename($model))->beforeLast('Resource')->snake('-')->plural()`. Meanwhile the canonical BroadcastsResourceUpdates trait passes `static::class` (the Resource subclass) so its channel comes from the registered $slug. Live repro (tinker): a Resource declaring `$slug='support-tickets'` for model Ticket — workflow-listener path emits `[private-arqel.tickets, private-arqel.tickets.5]` while the Resource/frontend path emits `[private-arqel.support-tickets, private-arqel.support-tickets.5]`. When a Resource uses a custom slug (the slug system's whole purpose; showcase Resources all set $slug explicitly), workflow state-transition broadcasts go to the wrong private channel and no subscriber receives them. The listener also has no access to the registry to map model->Resource, so it cannot derive the correct slug as written.

appMisuse.title: Audit activity-log endpoints leak cross-tenant + unauthorized record history (auth-only, no tenant scope, no Policy gate)
appMisuse.reasoning: Reproduced the surface: both endpoints are live in showcase via `route:list` (arqel.audit.activity-log GET admin/audit/activity; arqel.audit.record-activity GET admin/audit/{subjectType}/{subjectId}/activity), middleware = web+auth only, auto-registered by AuditServiceProvider->hasRoute('admin'). GlobalActivityLogController::index queries Activity::query() raw and returns every tenant's rows incl. properties; RecordActivityController::show accepts any FQCN (->where('subjectType','.*')) and returns full record history with no authz. The Activity model has no tenant_id column and no TenantScope (tenant scoping in this framework is per-model via BelongsToTenant, which Spatie's Activity intentionally does not use). No app-level middleware in showcase adds tenant/policy scoping. So a logged-in tenant-A user can read tenant-B Post history — a genuine, reachable cross-tenant exposure. HOWEVER: this is the framework's established and accepted convention, not a defect. The sibling export package (ExportDownloadController.php:18-30) ships the identical web+auth-only admin route with an explicit SECURITY NOTE that ownership/authorization is the consumer app's responsibility because the package cannot know the User/ownership model — and the prompt instructs treating that export case as not-a-bug. widgets/admin.php uses the same web+auth default. Audit follows this exact pattern; the showcase's failure to wrap the routes with a tenant/policy middleware is app-misuse against the documented convention. The only framework-side gap is that audit lacks the explicit security-delegation docblock its sibling export ships (a doc/hardening improvement, not a behavioral bug). Marking isReal=false per the default-skeptical bar and the explicit export precedent, classification app-misuse; severity high because the exposure is real and reachable if the consumer follows the convention without adding authorization.

appMisuse.title: LogsActivity logs ALL attributes (incl. password/remember_token hashes) for models without $fillable
appMisuse.reasoning: Code mechanics are accurate: getAuditableAttributes() returns ['*'] when getFillable()===[] (line 51), Spatie's trait expands '*' to array_keys(getAttributes()) ignoring $guarded/$fillable (vendor Traits/LogsActivity.php:229-231), and the wrapper never calls logExcept (logExceptAttributes stays empty, lines 234-237 no-op). So a $guarded=[] model carrying hashed password/remember_token WOULD log them. BUT this is not a reproducible framework bug: (1) The only model in the baseline using the trait is apps/showcase/app/Models/Post.php, which declares an explicit $fillable (title, slug, body, status, featured, published_at, meta, author_id, tenant_id) with NO password/remember_token — it never hits the ['*'] fallback. No framework-shipped model (auth User, tenant) applies this trait. The vulnerable scenario requires an app-authored $guarded=[] User model that ALSO opts into the trait; that model does not exist. (2) The sensitive-attribute risk is explicitly DOCUMENTED as intended behavior with app responsibility: SKILL.md line 83 lists 'Logar atributos sensíveis sem override' as an anti-pattern, stating override of getAuditableAttributes() is mandatory ('override é obrigatório') to mask password/api_token/PII before it reaches activity_log. The ['*'] fallback is asserted as canonical intended behavior in LogsActivityOptionsCanonicalTest.php:19-20 and described in SKILL.md line 16. (3) LogsActivity is an opt-in convenience trait; defaulting the whitelist to $fillable is a conventional, intentional design. Applying it to a $guarded=[] User without the documented override is the misuse vector. The chaining onto candidate #1 cross-tenant read is speculative and depends on an unconfirmed prerequisite. Verdict: documented, intended behavior; mitigation is the app's responsibility — app-misuse, not a framework bug.

notBugs.title: unique()/SlugField::uniqueIn() never injects ->ignore($record) — every unique field is unsaveable on update (collides with itself)
notBugs.reasoning:
The mechanism described is technically accurate and verifiable: HasValidation::unique() (line 78-92) and SlugField::getDefaultRules() (line 102-105) emit a static 'unique:table,column' string with no record id; ResourceController::update() (line 99-113) validates through the identical $this->validated() path as store(), passing no record; extractRules() (line 441) calls extract($resource->effectiveFields()) producing the static rule. A grep across the whole src confirms NO ->ignore() injection in the Resource validation path (the only ->ignore is ScopedUnique in the tenant package, which Field::unique()/uniqueIn() do not use; the only Rule::unique is hand-rolled in auth's RegisterRequest). So an edit-save of an unchanged unique value would 422 against its own row.

BUT this is explicitly documented deferred scope, not a reportable bug, on four independent grounds: (1) The HasValidation docblock (lines 25-29) states verbatim that unique() 'emits a unique:table,column string today' and 'CORE-006 will inject the current record at serialise time', and gives a documented workaround: 'pass it via rule(Rule::unique(...))'. The SlugField docblock points at the same deferral. (2) The acceptance criterion at PLANNING/08-fase-1-mvp.md:2341 ('->unique(...) gera Rule::unique() com ignore') is an UNCHECKED box — acknowledged-incomplete per CLAUDE.md's completion rule. (3) docs/tickets/current.md:750 and :729 explicitly track 'unique rule não inclui ID exclusion ainda ... virá com CORE-006'. (4) The showcase baseline never opts into uniqueIn()/unique() (PostResource uses Field::slug('slug')->fromField('title') only), so nothing collides out of the box — the repro requires the app author to opt into a feature documented as not-yet-complete, with a published escape hatch.

This is the same category as the EXPORT-007/008 signed-URL deferral the triage rules instruct me to reject: a known, documented, ticketed-but-unshipped feature with a stated workaround. Not a regression and not a latent defect. isReal=false.
